title: DevProbe Assist
preview:
Inbound from developer tools startup building an in-editor code assistant. Self-serve signup last week, ingested ~120k tokens during trial (backend + browser streaming). Team: CTO (ex-platform eng), 2x ML engineers, 3x frontend. Primary asks: stable hosted API with predictable burst limits, websocket/streaming SDK for low-latency token-by-token updates, long context (100k tokens) support or efficient chunking/embedding fallback, cost caps on per-session token usage.

CRM-style snippets:
- 2026-02-22: signup via website, trial credits applied, spike in token usage observed (trial session with 60k-context run).
- 2026-03-02: intro call (ff_20260302_devprobe_intro) — CTO: "need predictable bursts, can't have random 429s during demos". Wants explicit rate-limit tables and guidance for backoff.
- 2026-03-05: AE follow-up email with hosted API pricing + long-context guidance (thread-1a2b3c-devprobe-inbound).
- 2026-03-09: submitted SSO + audit logging checklist (drive:/security/devprobe-sso-reqs.md).

Technical notes / requirements (quick):
- workload: interactive code edits + multi-file summarization. Latency target: <200ms token start for editor streaming, tail latency secondary. Throughput: bursty (30 concurrent devs during demos), typical steady-state low.
- model preference: prefer open/quantized variants to lower cost but want Redwood-verified perf numbers.
- routing/fallback: want automatic fallback to smaller models on quota and clear policies.
- caching: interested in prefix/KV cache recommendations for multi-file context reuse.

Next steps / owner actions:
- SE (Priya) to run quick perf table for 32k vs 100k contexts with estimated token costs (due 2026-03-18).
- AE (Jordan) to schedule 60-min technical deep-dive (websocket + rate-limits) week of 2026-03-15.

Quote from CTO: "If we can't demo reliably with live editing, investors get nervous — need SLA-ish behavior for demos even if small scale."

Short-term blockers: pricing clarity for long-context sessions, official JS/TS streaming SDK example, explicit burst/429 guidance.

title: Cobalt Lane Technologies
preview: Founder-led discovery (AJ Moreno, CEO) — small SaaS building a developer-facing coding helper integrated in their web IDE. Very price-sensitive: 'we're spinning up >1M tokens/month in peak tests; need predictability.' Primary ask: clear line-item pricing and example invoices vs OpenAI/Anthropic (cost per 1k tokens, streaming vs non-streaming). Wants a quick POC on hosted API, using a 3-day trial cap and a predictable billing cap. Latency target: <200ms tail for 256-token responses, typical session 1–4 turns. Model preferences: open to high-quality open models or Redwood-verified variants if price delta is 20%+ vs OpenAI. Security: wants SOC2 and SSO mentioned; will request audit log examples. Notes from call: founder worried about hidden costs (context length, recalc of embeddings): asked for a sample monthly bill for 1M input tokens + 500k output tokens + 250k embedding vectors. Quote: 'If the math doesn't beat or at least match OpenAI for our workload, it's a non-starter.' Next immediate ask: send side-by-side pricing doc + sample invoice, plus short perf test plan (Priya to propose). Blockers: awaiting clear pricing sheet and three sample invoices. CRM shorthand: "founder wants apples-to-apples costings; prioritize pricing playbook + quick POC".

title: Copper Fern Digital
preview:
Inbound SMB lead from self-serve signup. Team: 4 engineers, 1 product owner. Primary goal: embed streaming chat into merchant support console with tool-calls to inventory/returns microservices and strict structured JSON for downstream order updates. Initial demo showed rapid latency and token cost wins on hosted API. 

Key pain reported: using 'JSON mode' (prompt-based) they get valid output most of the time, but in streaming mode tokens occasionally include trailing commentary or partial arrays that fail client JSON.parse. They also tried function calling (function_call:auto) but their function signatures differ slightly (optional fields, snake_case vs camelCase) and client SDK errors are bubbled up and confuse front-end handlers.

Customer quote (PM): "When streaming drops a stray "" or newline we immediately blow up our parsers — not acceptable for order update flows."

Dev notes: using Redwood JS SDK v0.9, Node 18. Sample prompt wraps response in explicit JSON markers but streaming still emits prefix text before first '{' intermittently. They have tiny POC dataset and can reproduce ~1/200 conversations. POC wants deterministic strict-json behavior or reliable function_call mode in streaming.

Suggested troubleshooting steps we gave: enable verbose SDK logs, toggle streaming buffer delimiter to '�' sentinel, set function schemas with required fields and exact keys, force function_call with name, and try 'json_mode: strict' on server side. They agreed to run with logging on and share console link.

Sales/ops: interested to stay hosted to avoid infra ops. Cost sensitivity moderate (SMB) — willing to commit if streaming stability proven.

Next meeting: 30m technical triage w/ Priya + Marcus + engineering lead (Evan Torres) to run repro on 2026-03-16.

title: Eudaimonia Health Co
preview:
Lead source: inbound via blog 'HIPAA-adjacent best practices for SMBs' - CTO (Kevin Huang) signed up and ran quick PoC with free credits.

Context: Eudaimonia runs a small chain of therapy clinics + telehealth triage. They want a conversational intake assistant to help reduce front-desk time and auto-generate visit summaries for clinicians. Not planning to send PHI in initial integration but want to be able to handle semi-sensitive notes.

What they asked for / voiced concerns: 
- "We need a clear answer on whether Redwood Hosted API will ever use customer prompts for model training" (quoted paraphrase from Kevin).
- Retention controls: "Can we purge logs? set retention windows?"
- BAA: "Not immediate PHI, but want to know path if/when we need BAA".
- Residency: prefer US-only logs, may expand to Canada later.
- Cost sensitivity: SMB budget, prefers predictable token pricing and quota alerts.

Quick wins suggested: share hosted FAQ + security one-pager, demo token-level cost breakdown in Console, show retention controls mockup. SE note: consider suggesting prefix-caching and short kv-cache TTLs for summaries to lower tokens.

Staging: discovery -> likely demo after SE session. Next: 30m technical call (wk of 2026-03-15) to validate retention flows and confirm no PHI in initial rollout.

Linked artifacts: fireflies ff:9b2a1c-2026-03-03 (call transcript), drive pricing + security docs.

CRM shorthand: inbound SMB, HIPAA-adjacent, public-cloud only for now, low-to-mid ARR, wants hosted_api, timeline: Q2 2026 decision if responsive.

title: Clarian Cove AI
preview:
Lead source: self-serve signup, small PMF-stage productivity app. Primary contact = CTO Aaron Diaz (quoted). Wants hosted API only (public cloud us-east-1) for now. Key ask: "What do we pick — Llama-2-13b, Mistral-7b, or Qwen-7b?" Wants a pragmatic list: quality vs cost, inference latency expectations, and recommended hosted SKU for 99th percentile cost. Use-case details: chat-centric customer support assistant + semantic doc search and occasional summarization for uploaded manuals. Expected volume: 2,000 DAU initially, peak 60 reqs/sec for chat during business hours, average turn length ~120 tokens, p95 latency target ~250ms for small responses. Cost sensitivity: initial budget target <$2k/mo while proving value; willing to expand after retention signals. Tried local Llama 2 playground and saw hallucinations on knowledge-base answers; CTO said "quality matters more than tiny cost wins if it avoids customer support errors." Still wants cost estimates for quantized 4-bit variants. Questions raised on call:
- How do Mistral variants compare on hallucination risk vs Llama family?
- Are Qwen models competitive for embeddings + chat bundling?
- What's the cost delta between hosted standard vs quantized endpoints?
- How does Redwood Optimize recommend batching/kv-cache settings for chat with 120-token rounds?
- Any recommended fallback routing (cheaper model for non-critical queries)? Wants a one-pager table: model | expected latency @ single-request | relative cost per-token | recommended use-case (chat/embedding/rerank). Also requested example billing projection for 2k DAU and 60 rps peak. Security: SOC2 okay, wants SSO later but not blocking. Next steps: 1) SE to prepare model-selection cheat sheet + simple cost projection (due 2026-03-09). 2) Offer small trial allocation of hosted tokens. 3) Schedule 30m deep-dive on 2026-03-11 to review numbers and plan a 2-week POC for chat + embeddings. Stubs: drive link for sample traffic and schema, gmail thread with CTO, Jira CLAR-POC-7 for POC tracking. Notes style: bulleted fragments, quotes from CTO, shorthand.

title: WillowStride Analytics
preview:
Lead source: inbound signup (product trial). CTO (Maya Chen) initial call 2026-03-02; wants low-friction POC on Hosted API. 
- POC scope: chat-based customer support assistant + semantic search for KB (50k doc corpus).
- Metrics wanted: <250ms p50 generation for chat; 40-60 reqs/sec sustained during business hours; token cost target ~$0.0015-0.003/token (very cost-sensitive).
- Model preference: open-source medium-sized models (7B-13B) if performance viable; will consider 70B only if latency and cost constraints met.
- Routing: prefer single-region (us-east) public cloud for simplicity; no current need for VPC/on-prem.
- Security: require SSO (SAML) and basic audit logs; not a heavy compliance customer yet.
- Procurement: default plan is credit card on file for self-serve; team asks if invoice is possible for early-stage startups (they prefer card but want invoice option if ARR grows).
- Discounts/credits: applied for startup program; asked if free credits can be extended (trial used up) for additional 60 days while they complete integration.
- Quotes: asked AE for indicative monthly spend at 10k messages/mo and expected token usage.
- Quote from CTO: "If we can keep cost predictable and avoid invoice paperwork for first year, we'll onboard quickly. Otherwise need finance sign-off."
- Notes on friction: billing questions slowing demo handoff to SE. SE Priya set to run sample workload once billing path clarified.
- Action items: AE to confirm startup discount eligibility and credit extension; finance to confirm invoice thresholds; SE to prepare perf runbook for 7B quantized model.

title: Honeycomb Sprint
preview:
Origin: self-serve signup via /signup (product-led). Founder: single dev (Colette Tran) building a collaborative writing plugin for indie writers. Wants fast iteration; initially testing hosted API only.

Highlights / context:
- Signed up 2026-02-28 using GitHub SSO, accepted free credits.
- Ran quick smoke tests with chat completion and embeddings for doc search (used demo dataset ~5k tokens).
- Implemented streaming UI prototype; reported p50 latency ~180ms on sample prompts (happy but wants to optimize).
- Hit token soft-cap on 2026-03-03; opened support ticket SUP-1123 asking for temporary quota bump — support replied with instructions (ticket linked).
- Billing: first card attempt failed (card provider issue), then succeeded on 2026-03-05 (one failed attempt noted).
- Prefers small, predictable cost; said on call: "I need cheap bandwidth for lots of short chats — price matters more than bleeding-edge quality."
- Model preference: open/verified models acceptable; interested in lower-cost fallback for routine queries and higher-quality for generation. Wants routing by cost/latency eventually.
- Security: no enterprise SSO yet; SOC2 compliance "nice to have" at scale, not blocking now. Data residency not required.

Recent timeline (bulleted):
- 2026-02-28: self-serve signup, auto-provisioned API key, initial test requests. (fireflies: FF-2026-02-28-78645)
- 2026-03-01: chat + embeddings integration; first search prototype working.
- 2026-03-03: opened quota/support ticket SUP-1123 after hitting soft limits.
- 2026-03-05: billing retry succeeded; invite flow tested for 1 teammate.
- 2026-03-06: user reported a streaming edge-case (truncation on long system prompts) and asked for best-practice doc.

POC / success criteria:
- Low friction onboarding: second dev invited and using API within 72 hours.
- Latency target for UI: p50 <120ms, p95 <350ms for typical short-turn prompts.
- Cost target: keep average token cost to < $0.01 per 1k tokens-equivalent for search-heavy workload (very cost sensitive).
- Feature targets: streaming, embeddings for semantic search, function-calling / structured output later.

Next steps (self-serve / growth ops):
- Send concise onboarding guide + code samples (node/python) (owner: Ethan Park).
- Share best-practices doc for streaming and prompt prefix caching (link to Drive doc).
- Monitor SUP-1123 quota bump; confirm when resolved.
- Encourage use of routing/fallback examples in hosted API to reduce cost.

Quotes / customer voice snippets:
- "Love how fast I got an API key and started building — saved me days."
- "If the price is right, we'll migrate 2k monthly active writers in 6 months."

Notes on commercial motion:
- Pure PLG lead; unlikely to need AE unless scale triggers dedicated conversation. Good candidate for nurture emails and community developer support.

Primary motivators: low-latency chat for agents (<150ms p95 ideal), high-quality embeddings for invoice/search, and deterministic reranking for suspicious tx alerts. Starting with hosted API to iterate; 'VPC-curious' once usage grows. Early security asks: SOC2 snapshot, SSO/SAML, audit logs, KMS integration. Cost-sensitive but willing to commit if latency and reliability stable. Quote from CEO: "We need predictable latency — not just average, p95 matters for UX." Trial shows sporadic p95 spikes during bursts; customer logged sample trace ids. They submitted Fireflies transcript for intro call. Wants a short eval matrix to compare Redwood hosted vs competitors on latency/cost. AE recommended Redwood Optimize suggestions for batching and cache.

title: FathomFlux
preview: B2B SaaS in-app chat assistant. Primary needs: low-latency streaming responses (target p50 <120ms, p95 <250-300ms under normal load), token-cost sensitivity for scale, support for function/tool calls and structured outputs, optional embeddings for contextual search. Prefer hosted API for time-to-value. Must support public cloud only (no VPC/on-prem at this stage). SSO/SAML desired; SOC2 in progress. Want transparent metrics (per-route latency + token cost).

title: CopperBasket Studio
preview: Inbound from founder (Elena) after reading blog post about cheap embeddings + QA. Small team, very product-led. Primary needs: product Q&A widget for product pages; auto-fill and enrichment for missing attributes (material, size guide); better search ranking during promo spikes. Latency target: page-load friendly, ideally <200ms P95 for first token. Peak behaviour: bursty around flash-sales, expected 100-200 RPS for 5-15 minutes, otherwise ~2-5 RPS. Cost sensitive — wants pay-as-you-go hosted API first. Quote: "Need something we can plug in today, scale on Black Friday if it works." Concern: token costs on high-traffic product pages; interested in caching suggestions and prefix caching. Request: sample cost estimates for projected traffic pattern. Shared drive links (pricing + quickstart). SE notes: catalog CSV has ~15k SKUs, inconsistent product descriptions; recommend embeddings + field-aware enrichment pipeline. No on-prem or residency needs now. Next demo should show streaming + search relevance tuning, include simple batching example.

title: Embercoda Labs
preview:
Lead: Nina Park (CTO). Small devtools startup building an IDE-integrated code assistant + repo search. Wants Hosted API (self-serve) — fast onboarding is key. Primary asks: 1) long context support (claims they need ~64k token windows for whole-repo snippets), 2) clear rate limit tiers and burst behavior (expected low QPS but spiky during CI/IDE events), 3) example SDK snippets for Node + Python showing streaming + function calling for code completions, 4) deterministic cost estimates for batching files and embeddings.

Recent call notes:
- "We need to surface whole-file context and preserve cross-file references when generating completions." — Nina.
- They ran a quick PoC against competitor X (small window) and want to compare latency and cost on longer contexts.
- Latency SLAs: interactive in-editor <300ms for short prompts; acceptable up to ~1s for heavy context completion. Throughput: baseline 2-4 req/s per seat, could spike to ~20 req/s during internal test runs.
- Rate-limit concern: want explicit per-token and per-minute limits + guidance on exponential backoff and retry semantics.
- SDK asks: Node + Python examples, code to stream tokens into Monaco editor, examples for chunked embeddings and prefix caching.
- Security: standard SaaS — SSO (SAML), audit logs, retention controls. No air-gapped/on-prem requirement at this stage.
- Budget: early-stage, price sensitive — expecting a starter committed tier or strong free credit runway. Estimated initial ARR $5k–$20k if economics look good.

Stubs/refs:
- Fireflies call id: ff_20260301_98234
- Drive: rw-pricing-proposal-v1.pdf (internal draft shared)
- Jira: RW-3812 opened to capture rate-limit doc ask.

Sales posture: PLG → AE-assisted (self-serve first, SE call to unblock technical reqs). Good fit for Hosted API; Dedicated/Private not needed now. Next: provide 10k/64k token example snippets + a concise doc that maps batching/cache knobs to cost delta. Follow up w/ SE tech call week of Mar 22.

title: Emberbrook SaaS
preview:
Founder-led lead: Ava Rodriguez, ex-early stage startup CTO. Very price-conscious; building a PLG support chat inside existing web app.

Use-case: chat-first support assistant + doc search embeddings for help center. Plan: start hosted API trial, then move to reserved if volume grows.

Quoted: "If you can't beat or match OpenAI price/latency I can't justify switching — but I like the observability and routing features."

Primary ask: clear apples-to-apples pricing comparison (per-token + embedding unit) vs OpenAI and Anthropic. Wants sample invoice for 250k tokens/mo and 1M embedding units.

Security: SOC2 required soon; currently SSO via providers (Okta). Need audit logs and retention controls. No air-gapped requirement.

Technical constraints: limited frontend dev time; prefers simple SDK + minimal orchestration. Needs streaming responses and function-calling for actions.

POC plan: 2-week trial with credits, sample prompts + eval set. If cost/latency good -> convert to paid self-serve then revisit Dedicated in 12-18 months.

Decision makers: Founder (Ava) + Head of Eng (contractor) — small buying committee.

Pricing sensitivity: low ARR now; forecasts $5k-20k ARR first year. Likely annualize at higher volume if cost advantages hold.

Next: prepare 3 scenario price sheet (low/typical/high usage), sample tokenized cost calc, and schedule 30m follow-up for pricing walkthrough next week.

title: BriefFoundry Legal Assist
preview:
Lead source: inbound signup (self-serve). Trial account created 2026-02-18 using free credits. Early discovery with CEO (Oliver Grant) + Head of Engineering (Priya Rao). Key context: small legaltech startup building contract summarization and internal advisor chat for SMB law firms. They ingest client contracts (confidential), sometimes PII. Prefer US-only data residency and want explicit audit logging for every request.

Highlights from call 2026-02-19 (ff_20260219_brieffoundry_intro):
- "We cannot have customer docs leave the US boundary; legal team will block otherwise." (Oliver)
- Interested in embeddings for semantic search across contracts + chat assistant for paralegals.
- Did quick smoke test of hosted API; liked generation quality but flagged two concerns: request/response logging and KV/prefix caching behavior.
- Asked if Redwood can disable request logging or provide redaction hooks and how long logs are retained by default. Wants retention policy that can be set per-account (legal wants 2-7 years for auditability but with ability to purge specific records).
- Wants confirmation that API keys and request payloads are not used for model training and options to route traffic to a US region endpoint.

Technical notes (SE follow-up items):
- Engineer asked about KV cache persistence: are prefixes stored long-term? Need doc on caching lifecycle and controls to opt-out.
- Request for example policy showing how to disable logging + enable audit logs to separate secure S3 with restricted access.
- Cost sensitivity: "Keep cost predictable — token-level billing + per-route dashboards would help us allocate spend to customer projects."

Behavioral / GTM notes:
- Self-serve first; likely to remain SMB with modest ARR but could expand if Dedicated offered later.
- Timeline: Legal review expected 2-3 weeks once we provide security FAQ + retention options. Forecasting conservative close if legal satisfied.

Stated next actions (recorded):
1) Send tailored security FAQ and short note on US-region endpoints and data residency (owner: Maya) — by 2026-03-06.
2) SE (Ethan) to demo audit-log config + show example quickstart that disables request logging and demonstrates per-route cost breakdown — schedule 60m deep-dive.
3) Provide sample DPA snippet and redaction/retention policy template.

Quote from Priya (engineer): "If we can control what gets logged and pin our data to us-east, we can move quickly — otherwise legal won't sign off."

title: Glowthread Works
preview: Inbound self-serve signup from solo CTO Elliot Ramos building a lightweight code-assistant for indie devs. Signed up 2026-02-20 and created API key. Wants hosted API only for now - 'don't want infra burdens, just reliable latency'. Primary workload: dev assistant (short-context code completions) + occasional semantic search via embeddings. Traffic: low concurrency, bursty. Latency target: median <250ms for short-turn completions. Cost sensitivity: high (hobby budget). Model preference: small Llama-family / Redwood curated small models for cost; wants fallback to larger model on edge cases. Requires streaming + function-calling for editor plugin prototype. No VPC/on-prem. Security: basic audit logs acceptable; mentions SOC2 if company hires. Quotes: 'If the API is predictable and cheap, I'll build the plugin and tell my indie circle.' Prototype: v0.2 VS Code extension, 3 alpha testers. Action items: send quickstart with streaming/function-call examples, pricing calculator, small-model recommendations, token budgeting tips. Offered 30% first-month credit in follow-up. Plan: nudge upgrade if token usage >50k. References: FF-2026-4491 transcript, thread-2026-8891 signup thread, drive://redwood/pricing/quickstart-glowthread.

title: CourtWise LegalTech
preview:
Lead: small legaltech startup building a secure search/summarization layer for plaintiff/defendant docs. Inbound via product signup -> support form. Wants hosted API (self-serve) if we can meet audit/logging needs and US-only residency.

Key pain: current search infra expensive, needs redaction + provenance for summaries when used in client deliverables.

Security reqs (customer quotes/highlights):
- "We need provable audit logs tied to user IDs, not just request ids."
- Must keep content in US region; SOC2 is nice but not a blocker today; retention policy controls required.

Cost sensitivity: SMB budget, low to medium ARR initially. Interested in usage tiering and developer-friendly onboarding.

Technical details discussed:
- Primary workload: embeddings + semantic search for confidential docs, and batch summarization for case briefs.
- Latency requirements: interactive search <200ms median; summarization can be async (30-60s).
- Throughput low (few dozen requests/hour early), anticipate growth if pilot performs.
- Model preference: open-models acceptable; prefer deterministic outputs and provenance.

Next actions: Ava to share hosted API quickstart + sample audit-log schema; Diego to prepare short runbook on recommended prompt/embedding settings and retention controls. Customer requested example logs and a short checklist for legal compliance.

Stated timeline: pilot in May, procurement in June if pilot OK.

Notes style: shorthand, bullets, call ids included above.

title: Foilbox IO
preview:
Founding team: Maya Turner (founder/CTO), 2 devs. Early-stage SaaS for developer onboarding flows.

Lead source: inbound self-serve sign-up (marketing blog + tweeter thread). Used free credits, implemented quick PoC hitting chat and embeddings endpoints.

Call notes (2026-03-01):
- Maya: 'I need a predictable monthly cost, not a surprise invoice' — very price sensitive.
- Primary comparison: OpenAI pricing + Anthropic for safety SLA. Wants explicit per-1M token price + embeddings/unit.
- Use-case volume: small to start (100-500 active MAU, ~5–10k tokens/day). Growth plan: x10 in 6–9 months if retention good.
- Latency target: interactive chat <300ms (pref <200ms) for US users; embeddings throughput low but need consistent latency for search indexing.
- Model preference: open to smaller open models (cost) but wants Redwood to show model-quality tradeoffs. Interested in Redwood model catalog and recommended defaults.
- Security: SOC2 important for investor conversations; asks if Redwood has audit logs + SSO for later.
- Deployment: public cloud okay, no VPC/private needed for now.

Founder asks / pain:
- Confused by token math vs prompt/response length — wants sample invoice for 10k, 50k, 200k tokens/mo.
- Wants to see concrete latency comparison vs OpenAI on hosted API for same model families.
- Asks whether Redwood offers onboarding credits or startup discount.

Committed items:
- Send pricing comparison spreadsheet (Ava) with three usage tiers + sample invoices.
- Include quick benchmark data (latency example) for comparable model variants.
- Attach security FAQ (SOC2 evidence, audit log screenshots).
- Offer 30-day trial extension or starter credits proposal.

Tone: founder-led, pragmatic, short decision cycle if pricing lines up. Prefers email + short async updates.

Stubs: Fireflies ff_20260303_001, gmail thread 1693749203847561234, drive folder for pricing.

title: HelpBloom AI Studio
preview:
Lead source: inbound — signed up via docs quickstart (Stripe trial). Uses: ticket summarization + structured output to populate CRM fields + tool-calling to triage and reopen tickets.

Initial qualification notes:
- Demoed hosted API (self-serve) on 2026-02-25 (call id ff-20260225-78a1).
- Primary contact: Lina Mora (Product Lead) — "we want something low-friction and cheap to start".
- Key metrics: 80ms latency target on short responses, ~10 req/s burst for weekday peak, monthly message volume ~40k tickets. Cost-sensitive, expects to optimize for token efficiency.
- Model preferences: open to Llama-2-ish variants; wants predictable cost and simple rollback path.
- Routing: single-region (us-east) preferred, no immediate need for private deployment.
- Security: SSO/SAML for console access, audit log retention for 90d, KMS later if POC success.

Recent timeline / activities:
- 2026-02-18: Signup via docs, trial API key provisioned automatically.
- 2026-02-20: Email thread with quickstart questions (thread-rdwb-20260226-1042).
- 2026-02-25: Discovery demo with AE/SE (Fireflies attached).
- 2026-03-03: Shared sample prompt set and token budgeting spreadsheet (drive link).
- 2026-03-05: Customer confirmed POC scope: summarize 5 ticket types, structured JSON output mapping to CRM, tool-calling prototype for reopen workflow.

POC scope / success criteria:
- Accurate summary (>=85% on intent & key action) across 5 ticket classes.
- Structured output matches schema with <5% parsing errors.
- End-to-end latency under 250ms median on typical tickets.
- Cost per ticket under $0.02 (target).

Sales notes / shorthand:
- Very PLG friendly; likely to convert if POC shows cost parity vs current internal lambda + small model.
- Main risk: will default to managed open-source hosting if cost looks better.
- "If Redwood gives simple batching + prefix caching examples, we'll be able to hit cost target" — Lina.

Next steps / owner actions:
- Maya: Send templated quickstart + recommended batching settings (today).
- Ethan (SE): Prep small-run canary script that demonstrates prefix caching and structured output mapping (due in 4 days).
- Schedule POC kickoff: target week of 2026-03-09.

Other refs: pricing deck (drive link), security FAQ stub (drive link), internal RDW ticket RDW-3471 for rate-limit question.

title: BrightQuark Marketing Engine
preview:
Inbound SMB lead from content-marketing platform. Use-case: generate high volumes of short-form marketing copy (emails, headings, social) + per-campaign personalization. Primary constraints: cost sensitivity and throughput. Wants hosted_api (self-serve) — "fast time to production" and minimal ops. Quoted prospect: "We run thousands of short prompts per minute during campaigns; per-token economics matter more than raw model accuracy."

Technical requirements / asks:
- Latency/throughput: target 200-300 rps peak, p50 latency target ~200-300ms for single-pass generation; willing to accept higher tail if batching helps cost.
- Cost sensitivity: heavy interest in batching + prefix/KV caching to reduce token spend; asked about suggestions for 8-bit quantized variants or cheaper open models.
- Model preferences: open-models preferred (Llama2-like family) for cost control; okay with Redwood-curated variants if price/latency trade-offs clear.
- Routing/fallback: want simple fallback to smaller model for non-critical creative variants and higher-quality model for hero copy.
- Security/compliance: SOC2 baseline, audit logging for admin actions; retention controls not strict but need clear docs.

Sales notes / talk tracks:
- Position hosted API as lowest friction; emphasize token-level cost breakdown and Optimize suggestions for batching.
- Offer a short tutorial on prefix caching and how to reduce repeated prompt tokens across campaigns.
- Proposed POC: 100k token trial over 7 days with sample campaign traffic + guidance from SE on batching config.

Next steps / owner actions:
- Ava to send trial enablement link + test credit on 2026-03-06.
- SE (Liam) to provide recommended batch sizes and sample SDK config by 2026-03-08.
- Follow-up call scheduled 2026-03-09 to review POC metrics and pricing options.

Current blockers: pricing clarity for sustained bursts, exact latency SLOs during campaign peaks, and whether caching integrates with their CDN pipeline.

Misc: Gmail thread includes example workload CSV + sample prompts (thread-17c9d2b8). Drive deck (pricing + security) shared. Jira INF-4567 opened to track POC infra questions.

title: Driftwood AI Integration
preview:
Lead source: self-serve signup (marketing site CTA).

Initial context:
- Small ecommerce platform (B2C) building conversational product finder + post-purchase support bot and personalized recommendations.
- Signed up with free credits 2026-02-18; poked around embeddings and streaming chat endpoints.
- Wants fast examples — 'give me a drop-in LangChain + Vercel example we can paste into Next.js' (quote from CTO).

Technical requirements / asks from call (2026-02-24):
- Primary integration targets: LangChain (Agents + RetrievalQA), LlamaIndex for document indexing, and Vercel AI SDK for edge serverless deploys.
- POC scope: index ~12k product docs (S3), power site search + contextual product chat.
- Latency target: ~<500ms end-user response for chat (95th pct) and <120ms per-embedding for single-vector calls (cost-sensitive).
- Throughput: bursty, ~20 req/s during promotions; mostly low traffic otherwise.
- Model preferences: open quantized models acceptable if Redwood hosted variants available; cost sensitivity — prefer smaller Llama-family or gpt-j-type quantized variants for embeddings/generic chat.
- Routing/fallback: want automated fallback to cheaper model during spikes.

Security / compliance notes:
- Needs SSO/SAML for admin users and audit logging. SOC2 compliance is a plus (customer security folks asked).
- No strict data residency requirement today; public cloud deploy OK.
- Requested documentation: security FAQ + endpoint level retention controls. (We shared /security/redwood-smb-faq.pdf — link in Drive).

Timeline & activities:
- 2026-02-18: self-serve signup, created first API key.
- 2026-02-20: AE outreach (Jordan) — intro email, asked about use-case. Gmail thread: thread:1782a9b2c5e.
- 2026-02-24: Discovery call (Fireflies ff_20260224_3112) w/ CTO + lead eng — demoed LangChain quickstart and streaming.
- 2026-02-26: Sent Vercel adapter quickstart + example repo link (drive folder).
- 2026-03-02: Follow-up: engineering hit blocker with SSO and uncertain about rate limits for embeddings; opened support ticket SUP-4128.

POC plan (customer-proposed): 2-week sprint
- Week 1: integrate Redwood hosted embeddings into existing LangChain retrieval pipeline; verify recall on product docs.
- Week 2: wire up Vercel AI SDK in Next.js to call Redwood chat endpoint (streaming) + run live A/B on product finder.

Ask list (what they want from Redwood):
- Sample adapters: ready-to-drop-in code for LangChain v0.1x, LlamaIndex indexing pipeline pointing to S3, and Vercel AI SDK example using Redwood-hosted_API with streaming.
- A short 'how to' on batching & caching recommendations to reduce token spend (suggested thresholds, KV cache hints).
- Clear published rate limits / burst guidance for hosted_api for SMB tier.
- Simple canary rollback examples / suggestion for model fallback config.

Sales notes / positioning tips for AE/SE:
- Emphasize quickstarts and small-code adapters — this team prefers copy-paste examples. Provide repo they can fork.
- Highlight cost controls (batching, quantization advice) and an easy path to Dedicated if traffic grows.
- Offer 1:1 pairing session with SE (Priya) to walk through LangChain + Vercel setup; propose a 60–90m pair-programming slot.

Next steps (agreed):
- SE to deliver LangChain + LlamaIndex + Vercel sample repo (Priya) by 2026-03-05.
- Jordan to schedule pairing session for week of 2026-03-08.
- Customer to provide SSO metadata for sandbox if they decide to promote POC beyond dev keys.

Open blockers (customer):
- Low internal engineering bandwidth — asked for minimal-effort SDK usage (Vercel example must be extremely lightweight).
- Need clarification on hosted API rate limits and per-embedding latency expectations.
- Security team requested SOC2 evidence and short note on retention controls (we promised drive doc).

Quick wins shared in thread:
- 1-page quickstart: 'LangChain + Redwood hosted API (Next.js + Vercel)' (in Drive).
- Small code snippet for streaming chat + function-calling included in email thread.

Activity links: Drive quickstart folder: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1Qv-driftwood-quickstart, Fireflies ff_20260224_3112, Support SUP-4128.

Sales priority: medium (SMB, cost-sensitive, but high chance to expand if POC successful).

CRM shorthand / follow-up items for AE:
- SEND: Vercel adapter repo + LangChain example (Priya).
- SCHEDULE: pairing session / code-sprint week of 03/08.
- TRACK: support ticket resolution and SSO docs submission.
- CLOSE: target small subscription (monthly) after POC; upsell path = Dedicated reserved capacity if traffic spikes.

title: DevSherpa Labs
preview:
Inbound from lead form: devtool startup building a context-aware code assistant. Wants hosted API + SDKs. Key asks: support for very long conversation/context windows (project-level context spanning multiple files), predictable per-user rate limits, easy TypeScript + Python SDK, streaming completion plus chunked embeddings. Interested in prefix/KV caching to reduce token costs. Team is cost-conscious but values dev DX. 

Call 2026-02-18 w/ Founder (Sami Patel) + CTO (Leah Park) — demo run-through. Quote from Sami: "We need the assistant to keep ~200k tokens of project context and surface relevant code snippets across repos. If the model forgets state, we lose trust."

Technical notes: primary cloud = AWS (us-east-1). Want simple API key flow for early beta, SSO later. Concerned about rate limiting for CI-style batch usages (hundreds of requests/min during indexing) vs interactive editor latency. Asked about SDK tree-shaking and ESM support for web-based IDE plugin. 

Trial ask: 30-day POC, start w/ hosted_api, ingest 10 repos (private GitHub) + run up to 150k token contexts in retrieval pipeline. Wants guidance on batching/quantization via Optimize suggestions. 

Follow-ups: send TS + Py SDK snippets, sample code for streaming + reconnect, cost estimate for heavy-context sessions, link to pricing deck and security faq.

title: Cascade Therapeutics AI
preview:
Account summary: biotech mid-market with heavy regulatory posture. High interest in private deployments for PHI flows. Long POC cycle expected because models will be used for clinical memo summarization and lit review.

Timeline (recent):
- 2025-11-12: Intro call (AE + CTO) — demo of hosted API. CTO liked latency profile; asked about on-prem options.
- 2025-12-02: SE session (Sofia) went deep on embeddings pipeline; customer asked about deterministic reranking and vector store compatibility.
- 2026-01-14: Product demo recorded (Fireflies ff-2026-01-14-ct-demo). Follow-up asked for security deck and HSM/KMS flow.
- 2026-01-28: Security handoff meeting — security team requested SOC2/ISO evidence + architecture diagram. Provided security FAQ link.
- 2026-02-10: Verbal commitment from Research sponsor (Sr. Dir. R&D) to move to pilot — asked for a dedicated instance and data residency in us-east-1.
- 2026-02-18: Sponsor resigned unexpectedly (internal reorg). Sponsor was main champion; recruitment frozen. AE notified by email (thread/1732c-procurement).
- 2026-02-20: Security team ping (Fireflies ff-2026-02-20-sec-review) asked follow-up on KMS/HSM and audit log retention. We responded; then radio silence.
- 2026-02-25: Last outreach (AE + SE) — no replies. Exec sponsor left; new org chart unclear.

Customer needs / requirements (from calls):
- Primary workload: long-document summarization of clinical notes, embeddings for literature search, reranking for SLR (systematic literature review).
- Latency: batch summarization OK (minutes), interactive research assistant target <= 800ms top-K response (for internal tools).
- Throughput: pilot ~50-100 requests/day, production could be 10k/day in 12 months.
- Cost sensitivity: medium-high; wants predictable committed pricing for Dedicated/Private.
- Model preferences: open-models preferred; allow quantization and pinned model versions.
- Routing/fallback: must support local fallback to on-prem model if cloud unavailable.
- Compliance: strict data residency (US), audit logs retained 7+ years, enterprise SSO, KMS integration.

Recent quotes/paraphrases:
- "We can't proceed without a security sign-off and a named sponsor." — Head of Research (on 2026-02-10).
- "If the sponsor leaves, this will tumble down the priority list until hiring completes." — AE notes after 2/18.

Next steps and plan:
- Short term: Put account in warm-stalled; stop active cadences for 4-6 weeks while org completes hiring/reorg.
- Request: Get an intro to interim owner (if any) or new Head of Research; offer a 30-min security deep-dive tailored to GxP/PHI flows.
- Long term: Re-propose pilot once sponsor is replaced; prep updated pricing for a 12-month dedicated pilot with KMS/HSM add-on.

Open items / blockers:
- No internal champion after sponsor departure (primary blocker).
- Security team still needs HSM integration spec and longer-term retention policy (SEC-1189).
- Finance hiring freeze may delay any procurement until Q4/2026.

Stale signals / risk assessment:
- High churn risk: sponsor left and department reprioritized toward internal ML infra.
- Competitive risk: they are evaluating in-house platform and may consolidate on existing cloud vendor (Vertex).
- Probability of close in current FY: low (<=20%) unless new sponsor emerges quickly.

Internal notes (AE/SE):
- Maintain concierge folder (drive:/deals/Cascade_Therapeutics). Keep security FAQ updated; add HSM spec doc.
- Skip weekly cadence; touch base after 4 weeks with a short note: "Hope hiring is going well — when ready, happy to re-run security session."

title: Emberfield Support Co.
preview:
Lead source: inbound via blog post (ticket-summarization + tool-calling). Signed up self-serve trial last week — used Hosted API free credits.

Initial discovery call (2026-02-12) with CEO (Lina Park) + Eng Lead (Omar Ruiz). Quick notes:
- Use-case: automated ticket summarization + structured extraction (priority, product, root_cause) to pre-fill ticket fields and drive triage rules. Also want tool-calling: ability to trigger ticket actions (assign, escalate, add tag) from model output.
- Workload: mostly short tickets (50-300 tokens), 3k–8k msgs/day now, expect 20–30k in 6mo. Latency target: median < 150ms for single-turn summary, 95th < 400ms acceptable. Throughput: bursty (morning spikes). Cost sensitivity: high — SMB budget, wants guidance on batching and prefix caching.
- Model preferences: prefers community/open models; ok with quantized variants. Wants Redwood-hosted variants only (no Private).
- Routing/fallback: asked about automatic fallback to lower-cost model under load. Interested in canary + A/B for evaluation.
- Security: SOC2 required for vendor, SSO (SAML) for admin portal, audit logs retention min 90d, basic KMS support requested. No air-gapped/on-prem plans.

Timeline & recent activity:
- 2026-02-10: self-serve signup, trial org created.
- 2026-02-12: discovery call (Fireflies ff-5a8c7d9b).
- 2026-02-15: trial usage spiked after running internal batching test (observed high per-token spend).
- 2026-02-18: emailed pricing question thread-u8Y2kzX2 asking about token-level discounts for summaries.
- 2026-02-19: SE intro scheduled; requested POC checklist and sample schema for structured output.

Quotes/snippets: "We need crisp, one-sentence summaries for agents + reliable entity extraction to map to our ticket fields." — Lina P.
"If we can keep cost predictable and show clear latency numbers, we can move to paid in a quarter." — Omar R.

POC ask (customer):
- 2-week POC using Hosted API: ingest 5k historical tickets, run summarization pipeline, return structured JSON with fields (summary, priority, product, root_cause, suggested_action).
- Demonstrate tool-calling flow: model suggests "escalate->tier2" and Redwood triggers webhook to create internal action. Need sample webhook flow.

Redwood recommended approach (internal notes):
- Provide quickstart repo + prebuilt prompt templates for ticket summarization and structured output.
- Example config for batching + KV cache to reduce cost on multi-turn agent followups.
- Share median/95th P99 latency benchmarks for similar SMB workloads.
- Offer 30m deep-dive demo with Marcus (SE) and Lina/Omar.

Next steps (short): send quickstart + sample schema by 2026-02-20; confirm demo slot; prepare small script to convert 1k historical tickets and run sample summarization with hosted_api credits.

Internal owner notes: Lina is pragmatic, budget-conscious. Prioritized speed of onboarding > advanced model tuning — emphasize turnkey templates and clear economics.

Stitch links: pricing deck draft in Drive, small snippet of Fireflies transcript attached to ticket INF-3421.

title: GrainWave Commerce
preview:
- SMB ecommerce platform building AI-powered product search and conversational support.
- Primary objective: replace current embed/rerank pipeline (Algolia + custom ranking) to reduce infra ops.
- Volume profile: P0: chat-based support (bursty, low latency), P1: embeddings for search (batch hourly), estimated ~150-250k tokens/month initially.
- Cost sensitivity: moderate — early stage, expects self-serve pricing to be competitive; will expand if unit economics hold.
- Security ask: 'Can you provide SOC2 Type II and list of sub-processors? Our GC needs DPA before billing/account upgrade.' — paraphrase from Rajesh.
- Data residency: majority NA, but ~8% EU customers; legal wants confirmation on whether EU data can be pinned to eu-1 region or if public-cloud multi-region is used.
- Preferred models: open-source fine if hosted by Redwood; want a small-latency model for chat (<=200ms p95) and embedding model comparable to OpenAI text-embedding-3-small.
- Routing/fallback: prefer automatic fallback to lower-cost model when latency SLOs are met, must preserve deterministic safety filters.
- Notes: they use Okta SSO (SAML) and require audit logs for API keys.
- Quote from Rajesh: 'We need the DPA and a simple sub-processor list — legal is the pace limiter here.'
- Internal: good fit for Hosted API self-serve path; low friction if SOC2 artifacts and DPA are provided quickly.
- Recommended pack: send hosted quickstart + SOC2 FAQ + standard DPA template; attach sub-processors CSV and point to console audit logs doc.

title: Apexhaven Intelligence
preview:
Status: STALLED — account went quiet after discovery.

Quick recap: Apexhaven is a mid-market ecommerce platform (personalization + conversational support). Primary ask: replace current ensemble (open-source + in-house) with a predictable, lower-latency serving layer that supports reranking and chat at peak traffic (p50 target ~60ms token-level, throughput ~500 concurrent chats). They prefer on-prem/VPC deployment for PII and residency.

Security: CISO escalation — asked for SOC2 evidence, KMS architecture, audit log retention policy, and a redline-able security appendix. Engineering is friendly to Redwood runtime (likes KV cache + batching suggestions) but security team flagged "no third-party key exposure" requirement.

Commercial: expected ARR band ~50k-150k; procurement indicated Q1 budget constraints — possible freeze next quarter. AE pushed Dedicated reserved capacity model. Competitors in play: Anthropic for hosted alternative, OpenAI for negotiated enterprise bundle, AWS Bedrock pilot with partner integrator (this latter came in after pricing discussion).

Why stalled: multiple factors combined — security review not closed, procurement paused due to budget cycle, and the internal champion (product lead) moved to another project. Outreach history shows engagement from engineering but not from procurement/CISO in the last 5 weeks.

Suggested approach: 1) escalate through SE to re-open technical convo with CISO (offer a 2-page redlineable appendix + short security Q&A session), 2) propose a constrained POC on a single region on Dedicated with strict KMS controls and short retention limits, 3) offer a limited-time pricing credit to bridge budget freeze.

Last quote from contact (Product lead): "If security can sign off quickly we'll re-prioritize — otherwise timing looks uncertain."

Current outreach plan: AE to follow up 2026-03-01; SE to draft security appendix by 2026-02-22. If no response by 2026-03-08 mark as closed-lost and ask for reasons (competitor, budget, security).

title: ClearFathom AI
preview:
Inbound from web signup - small fintech startup building a customer-facing chat summarizer for transaction histories. Self-serve trial started 2025-11-03; hit token spend concerns during early tests. Main ask: practical ways to reduce hosted API spend without sacrificing answer quality. Short bullets from discovery call:
- PD (product lead) quote: "We can’t absorb the token burn if every convo is 800 tokens". looking to get to ~150 tokens avg per convo or justify cost.
- Current approach: naive full-context prompts + embeddings fallback; experimented with 2-shot examples and long system prompts.
- Wants specific recommendations: batching strategies (combine small internal requests), prefix/KV caching for repeated account queries, prompt compression (slotting user metadata vs full transcripts), and a token usage audit of current traffic.
- Security: SOC2 in roadmap; currently cloud-only, prefers Redwood hosted API (no VPC) for speed. Concerned about log retention — asked how to configure redaction/export.
- Pricing sensitivity: SMB budget, likely monthly pay-as-you-go but may commit to a small monthly reserved bundle if platform shows consistent 30-40% cost reduction via batching/caching.
- Quote from CTO on call: "If you can show me realistic per-user monthly cost with optimization steps, we can convert from trial."
- Happy path: run a 2-week token audit + implement caching hint and prompt template tweaks, measure token delta; if >=30% improvement, convert to paid monthly tier.
- Wants example prompt templates, recommended batching thresholds, and an estimate of engineering effort to add prefix caching (client vs server).
- References attached: POC prompt set (drive link), current aggregated token dump request (awaiting from them).
- Sales note: keep communications short, technical-first; hand-off to SE for a 45-min optimization workshop.

title: Banyan Spark Finance
preview:
Lead source: inbound via blog post + free credits. CTO (Rhea Kapoor) demoed prototype last week.

Summary: small payments startup building merchant dashboard + reconciliations. Wants to add contextual AI for: quick transaction summaries for ops, automated support replies, and embeddings for internal docs. Mainly interested in hosted API to get started — 'fastest path to production' per Rhea. VPC-curious: asked about a lightweight VPC peering option later.

Security asks (early): SOC2 report, SSO/SAML for admin console, audit logs, KMS integration. Mentioned regulator in onboarding (money transmitter in 2 states) — not full enterprise yet but sensitive.

Performance targets: 50 rps peaks for support chat (p95 latency < 350ms desired), embedding queries ~200/day, reranking for fraud signals latency OK at 200-300ms. Cost sensitivity: high; wants predictable per-token guidance and batching suggestions.

- 2025-01-29: intro call (FF-20250129-4471) — demo of current flow, captured example prompts.

- 2025-02-25: CTO follow-up — asked about SOC2 + SSO; requested sample DPA.
- 2025-03-02: Discovery call with Maya + SE Jordan (FF-20250302-8492). SE captured sample traffic profile and latency targets.

- PM: 'If we can get SSO and an attestation, we'll move past legal.'

- SE Jordan to draft POC plan + token cost estimate by 2025-03-07.
- Schedule POC kickoff (30m) week of Mar 10.

title: GlacierBird Creative Suite
preview:
Overview: GlacierBird is an SMB marketing/content platform powering automated social & email campaigns for ~150 small brand customers. High-volume templated generation (social posts, short-form copy, email variants). They estimate 200k-800k generated items/month once scaled; unit cost matters. Interested in Redwood Hosted API for self-serve onboarding + Optimize suggestions for batching/caching recommendations.

Recent timeline:
- 2024-11-05: Inbound form -> signed up for quickstart. Assigned AE (Maya).
- 2024-11-08: Intro email + requested price sheet for high-volume tiers (see Drive pricing deck).
- 2024-11-15: 40-min discovery call (recorded, ff_2024-11-15_glacierbird_intro). Sec/ops lead + 2 devs attended. Discussed typical throughput and retry behavior.
- 2024-11-18: Dev follow-up asked for real token-cost examples for batching 20->200 requests and prefix caching options.
- 2024-11-20: Sent initial throughput targets and asked for sample POC keys.

Requirements / Signals:
- Workload type: batch content generation (templated), social_posting, email_personalization, light embeddings for dedupe/duplicate detection.
- Latency: per-request < 1s is nice-to-have for interactive authoring; bulk jobs can be higher.
- Throughput: bursts up to 1k reqs/min during nightly campaign generation.
- Cost sensitivity: primary constraint; looking to cut per-item cost via batching + prefix caching.
- Model prefs: open to smaller open models (quantized) for drafts, fallback to higher-quality model for final variants; want price/quality tradeoff guidance.
- Routing/fallback: interested in policy that falls back to cheaper variant under load.
- Security: SSO for admin console, audit logs for team activity; SOC2 optional but helpful. Data residency not critical (US customers).

Quotes / Call snippets:
- "We need predictable per-email costs — if it spikes we kill campaigns."
- "Batching + caching look like the quickest win. Give us numbers."

Linked artifacts:
- Pricing deck: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1GlacierBirdPricingDeck
- Security FAQ stub: https://drive.google.com/file/d/GlacierBird_Security_FAQ
- Internal thread with devs: thread-CA1234glacierbird

Next steps (short):
- Send detailed 1M-token cost scenarios (3 variants: naive, batched, cached).
- Provide a 2-week POC plan + temporary hosted keys for devs to test batching and prefix caching (requested).
- Quick follow-up demo week of 11/27 focusing on Optimize suggestions and how to measure per-item economics.

Blockers (short):
- Need clearer benchmarks showing token/unit cost with batching + caching.
- Legal wants a short note on data retention & audit logging before trial keys.

AE notes: Good inbound — marketing teams love UI and templates. Prioritize cost examples and a simple POC flow. Keep messaging concrete ("$ / 1k emails with X batching") and include fallback routing options.

title: Amberleaf AI Atelier
preview:
Inbound SMB lead. Tech profile: Next.js frontend hosted on Vercel, backend microservices in Node + occasional Python workers. Primary workload: interactive code-assistant embedded in docs + search/rerank for snippets. Wants low-friction hosted API (self-serve) — already on trial. 

Pain points observed: streaming behavior in Node examples inconsistent; LangChain \"chain\" wiring confusing for their team (mostly JS engineers); need LlamaIndex-style indexing example for product docs + a small-scale vector store (they use Supabase/Postgres but open to Pinecone). Cost sensitivity: chat-heavy, expects many short sessions per user -> asks about batching/kv-cache tips and token-minimizing prompt templates. 

Security/compliance: SMB but client-facing enterprise customers so wants audit logs and key rotation policies; will need a short appendix for their security team (SOC2 status, how to forward audit logs, KMS integration sketch). 

Quoted from call: \"We want a one-repo quickstart we can clone and drop into our dev pipeline — Node Next.js + a python worker for offline embeddings. If you give us a clear LangChain/LlamaIndex adapter and a Vercel streaming example, we can ship an MVP in 2 weeks.\"

Requested artifacts: LangChain adapter (JS), LlamaIndex example (py) using Redis/Supabase vector backend stub, Vercel AI SDK streaming snippet, sample cost analysis for 50k monthly requests.
